name: Schnecken (from Julia Naughton - Woman's Day)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the dough: Stir yeast into warm water with 1/2 teaspoon sugar. Let stand for 10 minutes.
2. Melt butter in a small saucepan. Stir in milk, remaining sugar, and salt. Heat until lukewarm.
3. Pour mixture into a large bowl. Stir in dissolved yeast and eggs. Gradually stir flour using a wooden spoon until a soft dough forms.
4. Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic. Place dough in a greased bowl and cover. Let rise for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
5. Grease 32 muffin cups (2 1/2 inches).
6. For the filling: Mix pecans, raisins, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a small bowl.
7. Punch down dough and divide into two halves. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one half of the dough to a rectangle measuring 14 x 10 inches.
8. Brush rolled dough with half the melted butter and sprinkle with half the filling mixture. Roll up jelly roll style, starting from the long side. Cut into 16 pieces.
9. Place cut sides down in greased muffin cups. Repeat with the other half of the dough. Cover muffin cups and let rise for 1 hour or until almost doubled in size.
10. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake rolls for 20-25 minutes. Serve warm. Makes 32 rolls. Per roll: 176 calories, 3g protein, 7g fat, 26mg cholesterol with butter or 14mg cholesterol with margarine, 122mg sodium, 25g carbohydrates. Percent of calories from fat: 5.7%.
